topic: Constructive identification in linear non-Gaussian SEMs under general non-factor confounding: for a direct-effect coefficient certified generically identifiable by the non-intersecting-path v-rank criterion of Tramontano-Drton-Etesami 2024 -- whose proof only produces a rank witness in the UNOBSERVED mixing matrix B_Lambda and yields no observable-side formula -- derive the EXPLICIT closed-form identifying functional expressing the coefficient as a rational function of OBSERVABLE higher-order cross-cumulants, via a cumulant-transfer that uses cumulant multilinearity and the bidirected connected-set vanishing pattern to cancel the general confounding at the cumulant order the graph dictates; this is the higher-cumulant generalization of the covariance IV ratio, valid precisely where second moments and linear-factor LiNGAM / over-complete-ICA identities fail once the parent edge carries a bow, with a consistent plug-in sample-cumulant estimator and delta-method inference as a plug-in corollary.

open_problems.open_problem: For every direct edge coefficient certified generically identifiable by the Tramontano-Drton-Etesami non-intersecting-path v-rank criterion under arbitrary bidirected error dependence, derive an explicit observable rational functional in joint cumulants rather than an optimization over residual independences or an unobserved B_Lambda witness.

source_refs.claim: The paper gives a necessary-and-sufficient graphical criterion for generic direct-effect identifiability under general non-linear confounding, but its constructive object is a B_Lambda rank system plus HSIC-style estimation heuristics, not a closed observable formula.
source_refs.author: Daniele Tramontano, Mathias Drton, Jalal Etesami
source_refs.venue: arXiv / submitted to Annals of Statistics

source_refs.claim: The ICML paper shows higher-order cumulants identify effects in single-proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM cases, but not the full ADMG/general-confounding v-rank class.
source_refs.author: Daniele Tramontano, Yaroslav Kivva, Saber Salehkaleybar, Mathias Drton, Negar Kiyavash

source_refs.claim: This paper gives simple cross-moment identification formulas for a single-proxy linear SCM and proves Gaussian impossibility, making it a low-dimensional prototype rather than a general graph-to-cumulant transfer theorem.
source_refs.author: Yaroslav Kivva, Saber Salehkaleybar, Negar Kiyavash

open_problems.why_unsolved: The TDE witness is a determinant/rank statement about directed path coefficients in the structural mixing matrix B_Lambda, while observed data expose only cumulant tensors of X after bidirected connected-set cancellations. Existing higher-cumulant formulas cover special proxy or IV shapes and linear latent-source lvLiNGAM, not arbitrary bows and connected error blocks in an ADMG with general non-factor confounding.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem assigning to each v-rank certificate finite index multisets A and C such that lambda_uv equals det K_X(A,C with the v-column replaced) divided by det K_X(A,C), where every K_X entry is an observable joint cumulant of graph-dictated order and the proof uses cumulant multilinearity plus connected-set vanishing.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest motivation arc: global graphical ID is too strict under bows, non-Gaussian generic rank criteria succeed, but the observable functional remains missing.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: global ID too strict under bows -> non-Gaussian generic rank criterion succeeds -> observable cumulant functional still missing

method_exemplars.technique: Cross-moment ratio identification and a matching Gaussian impossibility argument.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the cleanest low-dimensional analogue of replacing a covariance IV ratio by a higher-moment ratio.

method_exemplars.technique: Higher-order cumulant identifying equations paired with finite-sample cumulant plug-in estimation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It shows cumulant formulas can be operationalized statistically, but only for restricted proxy and underspecified-IV graph classes.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the minimum cumulant order needed to identify a v-rank-certified direct effect under a given bidirected connected-set pattern, and prove sharp non-identifiability from all lower-order cumulants.

source_refs.claim: The paper justifies genericity through cumulants truncated at arbitrary order, but does not give a graph-level minimal cumulant order for each identified coefficient.
source_refs.author: Daniele Tramontano, Mathias Drton, Jalal Etesami
source_refs.venue: arXiv / submitted to Annals of Statistics

source_refs.claim: This work uses rank conditions on matrices built from higher-order cumulants for discovery and local effect estimation, but not sharp order selection for arbitrary identified effects.
source_refs.author: Daniela Schkoda, Elina Robeva, Mathias Drton

source_refs.claim: The paper recovers bow-free acyclic path diagrams from suitable moments and residual dependence, but explicitly excludes bows and does not solve order-minimal effect identification under general confounding.
source_refs.author: Y. Samuel Wang, Mathias Drton

open_problems.why_unsolved: Higher orders can separate non-Gaussian sources and confounded error blocks, but sampling variance grows quickly and the available criteria are existential or algorithmic. No known theorem maps the bidirected component structure and path witness to the least cumulant arity that cancels every confounding term while preserving a nonzero determinant denominator.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A graph invariant k_star(G,u,v,W) and theorem stating that lambda_uv is rationally identifiable from cumulants up to order k_star, while generic models exist that agree on all cumulants of order less than k_star and disagree on lambda_uv.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames cumulant rank conditions as a practical alternative to overcomplete ICA and foregrounds graph-dependent cumulant matrices.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: overcomplete ICA is unstable -> cumulant ranks can certify local structure -> order-minimal effect identification remains graph-dependent

method_exemplars.technique: Finite-cumulant genericity combined with non-intersecting-path rank witnesses.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It contains the two pieces whose interaction must be made order-minimal.

method_exemplars.technique: Unbiased multivariate cumulant estimators with variance formulas.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies estimator-level tools needed once a minimal cumulant order is known.

open_problems.open_problem: Extend closed-form cumulant identification from one coefficient to simultaneous parent-effect vectors, giving stable determinant or linear-system formulas whenever the v-rank criterion identifies a whole row of Lambda.

source_refs.claim: The criterion reduces alternative parameterizations to row-wise linear systems involving B_Lambda submatrices, but does not expose observable cumulant matrices whose inverses recover parent vectors.
source_refs.author: Daniele Tramontano, Mathias Drton, Jalal Etesami
source_refs.venue: arXiv / submitted to Annals of Statistics

source_refs.claim: The half-trek criterion gives polynomial-time generic identifiability and rational recovery from covariance in linear Gaussian mixed graphs, but its observable equations do not use non-Gaussian cumulant cancellations.
source_refs.author: Rina Foygel, Jan Draisma, Mathias Drton

source_refs.claim: Latent-factor half-trek criteria certify rational covariance identifiability with dense latent factors, suggesting row-wise formulas but under linear latent-factor rather than arbitrary bidirected confounding.
source_refs.author: Rina Foygel Barber, Mathias Drton, Nils Sturma, Luca Weihs

open_problems.why_unsolved: A single cumulant ratio may generalize to a family of determinant quotients, but rank certificates can have many valid witness sets with different denominators and conditioning. Existing half-trek formulas operate on covariance trek systems, while general non-Gaussian confounding requires higher-order connected-set cancellations that must be coordinated across all parents of the target node.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A Cramer-rule style cumulant matrix equation M_X(G,v) lambda_pa(v),v = r_X(G,v), with M_X and r_X formed only from observable cumulants, plus a theorem that every full v-rank witness yields nonsingular M_X generically and all witness choices agree as rational functions.

writing_exemplar.why: It is a clear template for moving from graphical certificates to rational recovery of direct effects.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: covariance criteria identify some latent models -> latent-factor half-trek expands reach -> cumulant equations could expand beyond factor confounding

method_exemplars.technique: Trek-separation determinant equations and row-wise linear solving.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It provides the determinant-ratio template to translate into cumulant tensors.

method_exemplars.technique: Removable-ancestor row systems and non-intersecting-path rank criteria.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the exact row-level algebra that must be transferred to observables.

method_exemplars.technique: Joint matrix delta-method expansion for row-wise rational SEM estimators.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It handles simultaneous parent-vector inference for rational linear-SEM formulas, the same statistical shape expected after cumulant transfer.

open_problems.open_problem: When the v-rank criterion fails, determine whether observable higher-order cumulants imply a finite algebraic set, a sharp interval, or no bound at all for a target direct effect under general non-factor confounding.

source_refs.claim: Hidden-variable linear non-Gaussian models may have only a finite set of compatible causal models, but the result relies on linear latent confounders and overcomplete-ICA style recovery.
source_refs.author: Patrik O. Hoyer, Shohei Shimizu, Antti J. Kerminen

source_refs.claim: The paper identifies sets of causal effects compatible with observational data in latent-variable LiNGAM and gives uniqueness conditions, but not sharp cumulant bounds for general ADMG confounding.
source_refs.author: Saber Salehkaleybar, AmirEmad Ghassami, Negar Kiyavash, Kun Zhang

source_refs.claim: Complexity evidence and polynomial-space algorithms for generic covariance identification in linear SCMs show that failure of efficient criteria need not mean unboundedness or total noninformativeness.
source_refs.author: Julian Dörfler, Benito van der Zander, Markus Bläser, Maciej Liskiewicz

open_problems.why_unsolved: Generic non-identifiability can mean finitely many alternatives, a continuum with bounded target range, or complete lack of information. Current non-Gaussian work either returns finite ICA-compatible sets under stronger latent-source assumptions or binary identifiable/not-identifiable graphical certificates for the general-confounding ADMG model.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp partial-identification theorem describing the image of the cumulant moment map projected onto lambda_uv, with algorithms returning a finite polynomial fiber, exact rational endpoints, or a proof of unboundedness.

writing_exemplar.why: It explicitly treats compatible effect sets rather than only yes/no identifiability.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: latent variables break unique effects -> finite compatible sets are still useful -> general confounding needs sharp observable sets

method_exemplars.technique: Algebraic fiber and complexity analysis for generic identification.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It gives language for classifying fibers beyond identifiable versus not identifiable.

method_exemplars.technique: Moment equations with plug-in causal effect estimation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It shows how explicit moment restrictions can become estimable effect summaries when point identification holds.

open_problems.open_problem: Develop explicit higher-cumulant identifying functionals for direct effects in linear non-Gaussian SEMs with feedback cycles and general bidirected confounding, extending the acyclic v-rank picture.

source_refs.claim: The main results are acyclic; cyclic models get only necessary conditions, counterexamples to sufficiency, and a special subclass, with complete cyclic characterization left as future work.
source_refs.author: Daniele Tramontano, Mathias Drton, Jalal Etesami
source_refs.venue: arXiv / submitted to Annals of Statistics

source_refs.claim: This work uses linear non-Gaussian cyclic models for local causal discovery and strengths, but not arbitrary ADMG direct-effect functionals under connected-set confounding.
source_refs.author: Haoyue Dai, Ignavier Ng, Yujia Zheng, Zhengqing Gao, Kun Zhang

source_refs.claim: Half-trek generic identifiability does not require directed acyclicity, but it works with covariance mixed-graph parametrizations rather than non-Gaussian cumulant transfer.
source_refs.author: Rina Foygel, Jan Draisma, Mathias Drton

open_problems.why_unsolved: Cycles destroy the finite directed-path expansion used by acyclic B_Lambda arguments and introduce rational transfer terms from (I minus Lambda) inverse. Cumulant multilinearity still applies, but connected-set cancellations interact with feedback expansions, invertibility restrictions, and graph criteria that TDE show are no longer sufficient.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A cyclic cumulant-transfer theorem with determinant formulas in entries of observable cumulant tensors, valid on the open set where I minus Lambda is invertible, plus a graph criterion reducing to v-rank in the acyclic case.

writing_exemplar.why: It explicitly marks cyclic generalization as only partial after solving acyclic generic identification.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: acyclic ADMG criterion works -> feedback is scientifically common -> cumulant formulas must survive rational feedback transfer

method_exemplars.why_closest: It shows how path-rank arguments can be made meaningful without directed acyclicity.

method_exemplars.technique: Linear non-Gaussian cyclic local strength estimation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It provides a modern cyclic LiNGAM estimation analogue, albeit without a general-confounding cumulant formula.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive valid confidence intervals and influence-function or delta-method variance formulas for closed-form higher-cumulant direct-effect estimators under general bidirected confounding, including weak-denominator regimes where genericity nearly fails.

source_refs.claim: The paper focuses on point estimation and explicitly lists confidence intervals as future work, noting they could also diagnose violations of genericity through wide intervals.
source_refs.author: Daniele Tramontano, Mathias Drton, Jalal Etesami
source_refs.venue: arXiv / submitted to Annals of Statistics

source_refs.claim: The paper derives semiparametric influence functions and asymptotic normality for HTC rational covariance estimators, showing the analogous inferential gap can be solved once a closed rational estimator exists.
source_refs.author: Leopold Mareis, Nils Sturma, Mathias Drton

source_refs.claim: The ICML paper provides cumulant-based estimation methods for special lvLiNGAM proxy and IV setups, but not a general influence-function theory for arbitrary cumulant determinant ratios.
source_refs.author: Daniele Tramontano, Yaroslav Kivva, Saber Salehkaleybar, Mathias Drton, Negar Kiyavash

open_problems.why_unsolved: Closed-form cumulant ratios, once derived, are nonlinear functions of high-order empirical cumulants whose denominators are exactly the genericity witnesses. Standard smooth delta-method inference covers fixed nonzero denominators, but near-rank-failure regimes require a theorem connecting graph-genericity margins, high-order cumulant variance, and coverage behavior.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A limit theorem for the plug-in sample-cumulant rational estimator giving an explicit influence function and asymptotic variance away from zero denominators, plus a weak-denominator confidence-set or test-inversion result with uniform coverage over local genericity margins.

writing_exemplar.why: Its conclusion explicitly motivates confidence intervals as the next step after generic point identification and point estimation.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: generic identification gives point targets -> practical estimation needs uncertainty -> weak genericity should appear as wide or nonstandard intervals

method_exemplars.technique: Semiparametric influence-function derivation for rational linear-SEM estimators with matrix delta-method corrections.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It solves the same inference-after-rational-identification problem for covariance HTC estimators.

method_exemplars.technique: Sample higher-order cumulant plug-in estimation for identifiable causal effects.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the closest cumulant-estimator template in the LiNGAM effect-identification literature.

literature_map:
1. Tramontano-Drton-Etesami 2024 is the central anchor: it moves beyond linear latent-factor confounding to arbitrary bidirected error dependence and gives complete generic direct-effect criteria.
2. Its criterion is graphical and rank-based in B_Lambda; the observable-side rational functional is not supplied.
3. Classical covariance IV gives a ratio, but bows and Gaussian second moments fail exactly where non-Gaussian cumulants should add information.
4. Kivva-Salehkaleybar-Kiyavash 2023 gives a cross-moment proxy formula and proves Gaussian impossibility, making it the small-graph prototype.
5. Tramontano-Kivva-Salehkaleybar-Drton-Kiyavash 2025 pushes cumulants to single-proxy and underspecified-IV lvLiNGAM, but remains special-case.
6. Schkoda-Robeva-Drton 2024 uses higher-order cumulant rank matrices to avoid overcomplete ICA in discovery and local estimation, reinforcing cumulants as the practical observable object.
7. Hoyer-Shimizu-Kerminen and Salehkaleybar-Ghassami-Kiyavash-Zhang frame latent LiNGAM through finite compatible effect sets and overcomplete ICA, but assume linear latent-source structure.
8. Wang-Drton 2020 shows non-Gaussian moments can recover bow-free mixed graphs without knowing latent count, while bows remain a boundary phenomenon.
9. Foygel-Draisma-Drton 2012 and Barber-Drton-Sturma-Weihs 2022 provide the covariance half-trek/rational-identifiability template, but not cumulant cancellation under general confounding.
10. Mareis-Sturma-Drton 2026 shows inference can be built for rational SEM estimators after a constructive formula exists, sharpening the post-identification gap for cumulant formulas.
11. Doerfler-van der Zander-Blaser-Liskiewicz 2024 highlights the algebraic complexity of generic identification, relevant when v-rank fails.
12. The main unresolved tension is certificate versus formula: non-Gaussian graphical criteria now exist, but the desired output is an observable cumulant determinant ratio with plug-in inference.
prior_proposal_map:
No prior proposal directly solved the requested observable cumulant functional for linear non-Gaussian SEM direct effects under bows/general confounding.
The closest cumulant material is the LP/SVAR non-Gaussian fourth-cumulant family: useful for tensor-diagonalization, support gaps, and the recurring failure mode of assuming an unproved margin.
The closest rank/latent-completion material is the proximal and proxy-mediation bank: bridge nonuniqueness, observable thresholds, Farkas separators, and finite response-type sharpness.
The leaky-frontdoor and multi-mediator entries are useful for shared-latent coupling, bilinear compatibility, finite-vertex endpoints, and negative saturation results.
The anchor/ICP/Dantzig entry is the main linear SEM/environment comparator, but it failed because its frontier was definitional stacking rather than independent minors.
The MPDAG entry warns that pure graph identification certificates are often already solved; nearby novelty should be observable functionals, inference, or efficiency.
Across mined sources, the reusable pattern is: convert an unobserved algebraic/rank certificate into an observable functional or finite polynomial certificate, and prove sharpness rather than assuming it.
